title: Modelmeter Pricing Snapshot
description: Versioned pricing data for AI models. All monetary values are USD per million tokens (per Mtok).

snapshot_date.description: ISO date the snapshot represents (not the date a price was last verified).

id.description: Stable identifier: provider/model. Used as the API param.

provider.description: Provider identifier in lowercase-hyphenated form. Common: anthropic, openai, google, xai, venice, openrouter, deepseek, mistral, cohere, alibaba, zhipu, together, fireworks, groq, deepinfra. Not enum-constrained so new providers can be added without a schema bump.

input_cost_per_mtok.description: USD per 1M input tokens. null = not yet verified.

output_cost_per_mtok.description: USD per 1M output tokens. null = not yet verified.

cache_write_cost_per_mtok.description: USD per 1M tokens written to prompt cache. null if not applicable or unverified.

cache_read_cost_per_mtok.description: USD per 1M tokens read from prompt cache. null if not applicable or unverified.

last_verified.description: ISO date a human last confirmed these values match the source page.

verification_required.description: true means a human has NOT confirmed any values in this entry yet. Calculators should refuse to return results for entries marked true.

deprecated_on.description: ISO date the provider deprecated this model, or null if still available.

availability.description: Current usability of the model. 'available' = freely usable via the provider's standard API; 'restricted' = limited access (enterprise-only, waitlist, region-gated); 'unavailable' = cannot currently be used (regulatory action, suspended, etc.); 'deprecated' = provider has sunset the model. Reason should be explained in `notes`.

tags.description: Optional flags describing the model. Common values: 'beta', 'experimental', 'e2ee' (end-to-end encrypted), 'private', 'vision', 'thinking' (reasoning model), 'coder', 'uncensored', 'fast', 'pro', 'turbo', 'flash', 'mini'. Use lowercase. Not enum-constrained to allow future tags without schema bumps.

upstream_model_id.description: For reseller entries (e.g., Venice reselling Claude), the `id` of the direct provider's entry. Lets consumers compute markup. Example: a Venice entry for Claude Opus 4.8 sets this to 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-8'. Null for direct entries and for entries with no direct equivalent (open-source models hosted via Venice, Venice-exclusive models).

aliases.description: Alternate ids that resolve to this model in lookups (e.g. /model, MCP get_model). Store the provider's exact API model ids here — the bare id without our 'provider/' prefix — when they differ from our canonical `id`. Example: our 'xai/grok-4-20' lists ['grok-4.20-0309-reasoning', 'grok-4.20-0309-non-reasoning']; 'mistral/mistral-large-3' lists ['mistral-large-latest']. Matching is exact, then case/dot-normalized. Each alias must be unique across all models.

above_input_tokens.description: When input tokens exceed this threshold, the rates below apply.

tier_pricing.description: Tiered overage pricing. Some providers charge a higher rate when input context exceeds a threshold (e.g., Venice's GPT-5.5 doubles above 272K input tokens). Each tier overrides the top-level rates when its threshold is met.
